description_long: This office building features a stepped, tiered structure with five visible floors, each recessed slightly from the one below, creating a ziggurat-like silhouette. The facade is dominated by rows of rectangular windows, evenly spaced and horizontally aligned, giving the building a strong sense of regularity and rhythm. The exterior appears to be constructed from concrete or a similar material, with a uniform light gray color throughout. A prominent entrance ramp leads up to a recessed entryway at the base, emphasizing accessibility and a formal approach. The roof is flat and slightly sloped, consistent with modernist and brutalist architectural influences. There is no visible signage or decorative detailing, reinforcing the utilitarian and functional aesthetic.
description: A stepped, tiered office building with five floors, uniform rows of rectangular windows, and a prominent entrance ramp, all in a light gray concrete finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance ramp and recessed entryway, with the stepped facade and rows of windows clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tiered structure and horizontal window bands, with the entrance ramp extending to the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the stepped design and window pattern, but without any visible entrance or ramp.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, showing the tiered floors and window rows, with the entrance ramp extending to the right.
attribute_window_pattern: Regular horizontal bands of rectangular windows, evenly spaced and repeated across each floor.
